title: Little Sloan Leaning Bookshelf
summary: A wall-mounted ladder frame at a 10° lean holds three staggered plywood boxes — the angle is the whole design, not a decoration on top of a square shelf.
description:
The leaning frame isn't cosmetic — every frame piece is cut to a matching 10° angle on both ends, parallel to each other, which is what lets the whole ladder lean back convincingly instead of just looking like a crooked square shelf. Cut the frame pieces as a matched set before you touch the boxes, and don't mix a piece from one angle setting with a piece from another; a few degrees of drift between pieces is very visible on a leaning silhouette.

The three storage boxes are sized differently on purpose — small, medium, large, stepping up as they go down the ladder — and this is a mounted piece, not free-standing: the frame anchors to wall studs and carries real load, so don't skip the stud-mounting step or treat it as optional the way you might on a floor shelf.

tools.note: Every frame piece needs a matching 10° angle on both ends — set it once and cut the whole set.

materials.name: Plywood, 3/4"
materials.note: At least 15-1/2" x 31" — can substitute MDF or a project panel.

materials.name: Lag screws, 3" or longer
materials.note: Anchors the frame to wall studs.

cutList.note: Both ends cut at 10° off square, ends parallel, long-point-to-short-point measurement.
Both ends cut at 10° off square, ends parallel, long-point-to-short-point measurement.
Both ends cut at 10° off square, ends parallel.
Both ends cut at 10° off square, ends parallel.

steps.title: Build the two leg sets as mirror images
steps.body: Attach the spacer pieces to the full-length leg boards to build the two leg sets — build them IN MIRROR, not as identical copies. Before you fasten anything permanently, check the gap between spacers is wide enough to slide your boxes in easily; it's much easier to widen a gap now than after the frame is assembled.

steps.title: Join the frame with top and bottom
steps.body: Attach the top and bottom spacers to the two leg sets to complete the leaning ladder frame.

steps.title: Build the shelf and boxes
steps.body: Build the top shelf and the two storage boxes using either 2" finish nails or 3/4" pocket holes with 1-1/4" pocket screws, glued at every joint.

steps.title: Mount the frame and add the shelf and boxes
steps.body: Mount the frame to the wall, driving the lag screws into studs — this piece carries real load and depends on a stud mount, not just drywall anchors. Once the frame is secure, slide the shelf and boxes into place and screw them to the frame sides.

images.alt: Wall-mounted leaning bookshelf with a 10-degree angled frame and three staggered storage boxes
